title: [v4.2.0-rhel] Backport fixes for cve-2025-52881 cve-2025-58183 cve-2025-47913 cve-2025-65637 cve-2025-47913
body:
Backport commits from #27987 (v4.4.1-rhel) to v4.2.0-rhel.

Fixes: https://issues.redhat.com/browse/RHEL-126909 https://issues.redhat.com/browse/RHEL-132853 https://issues.redhat.com/browse/RHEL-132863 https://issues.redhat.com/browse/RHEL-126907 https://issues.redhat.com/browse/RHEL-132832 https://issues.redhat.com/browse/RHEL-132860 https://issues.redhat.com/browse/RHEL-125714 https://issues.redhat.com/browse/RHEL-134785 https://issues.redhat.com/browse/RHEL-134781 https://issues.redhat.com/browse/RHEL-135819 https://issues.redhat.com/browse/RHEL-135805 https://issues.redhat.com/browse/RHEL-134785 https://issues.redhat.com/browse/RHEL-134781

**Note:** This backport was performed with the assistance of AI.  Though most of the cherry-picks were simple/straight-forward, a LOT of updates were required (for the project to compile) subsequent to the **_Adjust for common bump_** commit.

As most of this work was duplicate definition/function removal, AI-assistance was heavily utilized.  Further, in order to smooth the later cherry-picking, I instructed the AI agent to also make updates necessary to align better with the 4.4.1 branch WRT #27987

All these changes were added in the **_Fix compilation errors for v4.4.1-rhel compatibility_** commit, with a (generated) summary of what was done.  **Every** commit after this one was checked for project compile-ability with the exception of the break/fix series near the end--

- Ignore SELinux relabel on unsupported file systems (break)
- Add missing unix import for ENOTSUP (fix)

#### Checklist

```release-note

```

comments.body:
Note to me: Main divergence with #27987 (based on titles only) after (ours) "Fix storage container mount check to fail on errors" should be (theirs) "Fix integration test build failures".  Ignoring (ours only):
- [Fix compilation errors for v4.4.1-rhel compatibility](https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/27933/commits/bb83ffc1fd23cccc4b81ff28f2f0b6e1e272e7bc)
- [test/system: fix mount/unmount test for external containers](https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/27933/commits/eaf956651938b1a5ff45d3f771cd8f8f6475bdd5) (still broken?)
- [test/system: add helpers.network.bash and load statements](https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/27933/commits/15c5e29671cc44121e39b736fe805c7a7770b7e3)
I guess like the 4.4.1-rhel PR, it's downright infeasible to reliably review it. So, as long as build and tests work well, LGTM from me.
Status: I just completed a RHEL build for buildah 1.26, that was necessary to properly run tests on this PR.  But I'm on PTO for the rest of the week, so will resume work here next week.

RHEL local-only test results.  They don't look good, but the good news is root and rootless agree on many of the failures :grin: 

- [4566280-local_rootless_system.txt](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/25717611/4566280-local_rootless_system.txt)
- [4566280-local_root_system.txt](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/25717612/4566280-local_root_system.txt)

The RHEL system-test situation has improved, but I'm still getting failures in rootless for: [20e2c75-local_rootless_system.txt](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/25801556/20e2c75-local_rootless_system.txt) 

- podman build
- many events tests
- podman login
- selinux (I think these are simply broken on RHEL)

And rootful: [20e2c75-local_root_system.txt](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/25801555/20e2c75-local_root_system.txt)

- podman build
- many events tests
- podman run CONTAINERS_CONF dns options (passes for rootless?!?!?!)
- selinux (I think these are simply broken on RHEL)

I was hoping that including [f3e8fbd](https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/27933/commits/f3e8fbd523731abd0a37c978f6d4e9743946fed5), [701d5af](https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/27933/commits/701d5af272dfffb4a233eb373b7ef09c5f3cd6d2), and 20e2c75 would have addressed the "CONTAINERS_CONF dns options" test :thinking: 

@lsm5 do you have any hints/ideas on this?  According to AI the feature was intended for implementation in v4.2.0, but it boggles my mind why it works for rootless and not root.  I attempted to let AI do some debugging but it ended up slopping all over the place :confounded: so I reverted its "efforts".

---

Notes from commit spelunking:
- commit 1bcf382 backports b7ab889a from PR #16297.
  needed because much of that PR already came in via...
- a --theirs resolution in 9b1c3286 in which AI 
  created container_internal_common.go from whole-cloth
  rather than making a one-line change :S
- Consider rebase-fixing 9b1c3286 to undo the AI sloppiness. 

---

Edit: This problem seems to be caused by the RHEL 9.2 version of netavark missing a critical commit.  So disabling the test is probably the most sensible thing to do :disappointed:
Update: I've moved on to work on the set of "podman events" test failures.

Update: All of the local root, rootless, and remote rootless tests are passing now.  There are just 4 failures in remote root and at least one of them I'm 99% sure was also a problem in v4.4.1-rhel and we chose to ignore it.

[ed2f77a-remote_root_system.txt](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/26033599/ed2f77a-remove_root_system.txt)

Update: All system tests are passing now.  The only exception is the same ignored test (also in v4.4.1-rhel), where podman-remote (somehow) still connects to the "server" even after the named default connection is removed.  I believe this to be a test bug or environment issue.  Anyway, onward to bigger and more exciting things...

> I think we should run integration and other tests too if we have the time.

It was due over 3 months ago, so a few more days is of little consequence IMHO.  I'm uncomfortable about the idea of it going on for weeks and weeks longer though.  If you could help run those, or help me run those I'd greatly appreciate it -- It may come as a shock to learn, I've never run them outside of CI before :blush: 

> btw, Is this meant to be on RHEL 9.0.0.z ?

Correct, and RHEL 8.6.0.z.  It may also be used as the basis for a RHEL 8.4.z / podman v3.2.3 backport :scream:

Though on second-thought...that version is so incredibly old, it may actually be less work if we only bring back the "[Bump runc to 1.2.9, Buildah 1.29.6](https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/27933/commits/2ba0070cc4d2f49b7d95678566328e71e884843f)" commit, and move forward from there until it compiles & passes tests :thinking:

> > I think we should run integration and other tests too if we have the time.
> 
> It was due over 3 months ago, so a few more days is of little consequence IMHO. I'm uncomfortable about the idea of it going on for weeks and weeks longer though. If you could help run those, or help me run those I'd greatly appreciate it -- It may come as a shock to learn, I've never run them outside of CI before 😊

I spawn a testing-farm instance on the internal ranch using `testing-farm reserve ...` and then just clone the fork and run all tests manually. Maybe I could give this a go early tomorrow.

Then again, if we find out the other tests are failing and need adjustments, how much longer are you willing to spend on those?

> Then again, if we find out the other tests are failing and need adjustments, how much longer are you willing to spend on those?

This is the operative question.  This fix needs to go out sooner rather than later.  Unless the tests find some major SNAFU, I'd prefer to fix via followup PR.

@lsm5 I attempted to make the e2e tests run but am hitting a roadblock I don't know how to overcome.  First of all, the ginkgo parts of the `Makefile` and more importantly the `test/tools` sub-directory on this branch are both full of bit-rot.  I attempted to checkout `test/tools` from the v4.4.1-rhel branch and that at least got ginkgo built and installed.  But when the tests try to run, they immediately fail with:

```
[root@cevich-1mt-rhel-9 podman]# make localintegration
...cut...
ACK_GINKGO_RC=true ./test/tools/build/ginkgo version
Ginkgo Version 1.16.5
ACK_GINKGO_RC=true ./test/tools/build/ginkgo -v  -tags "exclude_graphdriver_btrfs btrfs_noversion selinux systemd libsubid exclude_graphdriver_devicemapper seccomp remote" -timeout=90m -cover -flakeAttempts 3 -progress -trace -noColor -nodes 3 -debug test/e2e/. hack/.
Failed to compile e2e:

# github.com/containers/podman/v4/test/e2e
package github.com/containers/podman/v4/test/e2e (test)
        imports github.com/containers/buildah
        imports github.com/containers/common/libimage: build constraints exclude all Go files in /root/podman/vendor/github.com/containers/common/libimage
FAIL    github.com/containers/podman/v4/test/e2e [setup failed]
FAIL

Ginkgo ran 1 suite in 1.57928407s
Test Suite Failed
make[1]: *** [Makefile:553: ginkgo-run] Error 1
make[1]: Leaving directory '/root/podman'
make: *** [Makefile:557: ginkgo] Error 2
```

Some backports pointed out by claude

-----

1. `exec --privileged` (Need to backport: commit 68b2450d3, 6668ac93b)

  bb83ffc1fd replaced setProcessCapabilitiesExec() with a simplified 3-line inline check:
```
  if pspec.Capabilities == nil {
      pspec.Capabilities = c.config.Spec.Process.Capabilities
  }
```

  This only copies container capabilities when pspec.Capabilities is nil. It doesn't handle --privileged exec (which needs the full bounding set) and doesn't handle the case where a non-root exec user matches the container user (which needs ambient/inheritable caps set). The original method handles both cases
  properly.

Without this fix, failures in exec and run integration tests.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  
                                 

------

2. `Notify socket` (Need to backport: be5d1261b)

  - mountNotifySocket() references c.notifySocket — a runtime struct field that's never populated. It should use c.config.SdNotifySocket, which is the persisted config field that survives across container restarts.
  - WithSdNotifySocket() option function is entirely absent from libpod/options.go, so there's no way to set c.config.SdNotifySocket during container creation.
  - container_create.go never captures NOTIFY_SOCKET from the environment, so even if the option function existed, it would never be called.

  The net effect is that NOTIFY_SOCKET is never forwarded into containers, breaking any systemd service that relies on sd-notify readiness signaling.

Without this fix, failure in `podman run notify_socket`  integration test.

------

Apart from that, compose and docker api v2 tests passed. Other integration test failures noticed on this PR were also noticed on v4.2.0-rhel branch so shouldn't be an issue here.

Well at least the "remote" tests passed, all the local tests failed with quay.io access problems (that obviously cleared up later in the run) :disappointed: 

- [45aff3b-remote-rootless.txt](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/26381961/45aff3b-remote-rootless.txt)
- [45aff3b-remote-root.txt](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/26381965/45aff3b-remote-root.txt)

Re-executing the "local" tests again...

The "local" tests finished, everything ran clean.

- [45aff3b-local-rootless.txt](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/26386114/45aff3b-local-rootless.txt)

@TomSweeneyRedHat @lsm5 @dashea PTAL and merge as appropriate (I realize Lokesh is out this week -- this can wait for him to get back if needed).

Oh crud!  I thought I left a comment here last week, I must have written it up, and neglected to hit the comment button.

In short, I think you need to add a commit for: https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/27652

I'm fine if it's done as a follow up.

LGTM
Thanks @TomSweeneyRedHat and @lsm5.  I'll open another PR shortly for the https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/27652 backport.  Thanks for the reminder.
